title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa

seo.title: Chilaquiles Rojos Recipe: Authentic Mexican Breakfast
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chilaquiles Rojos with our easy step-by-step recipe. Perfect for a delicious Mexican breakfast or brunch. Get the best red salsa chilaquiles!
h2Headings: Ingredients for Chilaquiles with Red Salsa
How to Make Chilaquiles Rojos: Step-by-Step
seo.imageAlt: Plate of authentic Chilaquiles Rojos with fried eggs and crema
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a vibrant plate of homemade Chilaquiles Rojos, a classic Mexican breakfast.

description: Chilaquiles Rojos are a quintessential Mexican breakfast, transforming humble corn tortillas into a magnificent feast bathed in a rich, spicy red salsa. This heartwarming dish offers a symphony of textures and tastes, making it the perfect start to any day with its comforting warmth and bold flavors. Every bite is an explosion of authentic Mexican cuisine.

ingredients.notes: or jalapeños, stemmed, adjust to taste

instructions.title: Prepare the Salsa Base
instructions.text: Place the chopped Roma tomatoes, serrano chiles, garlic cloves, and roughly chopped white onion in a medium saucepan. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the tomatoes are soft and the chiles have changed color. Drain most of the water, reserving about 1/4 cup.

instructions.title: Blend the Red Salsa
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooked vegetables to a blender. Add the chicken broth and the reserved cooking water. Blend until smooth. For a very smooth salsa, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve, but it's optional. Set aside.

instructions.title: Fry the Tortilla Chips
instructions.text: Heat 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Once hot, fry the tortilla quarters in batches until golden brown and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per batch. Do not overcrowd the pan. Transfer the fried chips to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil. Season lightly with salt.

instructions.title: Simmer the Red Salsa
instructions.text: In the same large skillet (after draining most of the oil, leaving about 1 tablespoon), pour the blended red salsa. Bring it to a simmer over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Season with salt to taste. Let it simmer for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and the salsa to thicken slightly.

instructions.title: Combine Chips and Salsa
instructions.text: Add the fried tortilla chips directly into the simmering red salsa. Stir gently to ensure all the chips are coated. Cook for 2-3 minutes, just until the chips begin to soften but still retain some texture. Do not overcook, or they will become soggy.

instructions.title: Serve Chilaquiles Rojos
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the Chilaquiles Rojos to individual plates. Garnish generously with a drizzle of crema Mexicana, a sprinkle of crumbled queso fresco, thinly sliced red onion, and fresh chopped cilantro. For an extra hearty breakfast, top with a perfectly fried egg.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, try using day-old corn tortillas to make your own chips. They absorb the salsa better and have a superior texture.
Adjust the spiciness by increasing or decreasing the number of serrano chiles. For a milder salsa, remove the seeds and veins from the chiles before boiling.
Serve immediately after combining the chips and salsa to prevent the tortillas from becoming too soggy. The perfect Chilaquiles have a balance of soft and slightly firm chips.
article: Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a beloved Mexican Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
